stairs.description: Floor-spanning stairs. A sibling of `floors` because each stair connects two floors; see the design specification, sections 3.1 and 3.2.

roomOverrides.description: Per-room user metadata keyed by `roomKey(room)`. A sibling of `meta` and `floors` so an undoable command can reassign it whole (the inverse-capture proxy records only the root's own top-level keys). Absent means no overrides.

palettes.description: Project-local, user-editable palettes (design spec 3.1). Absent means none.

paint.description: Surface paint assignments keyed by surfaceKey(ref). Absent means none.

site.description: Optional site metadata (design spec 3.1). Absent means none.

period.description: The project's default chronological period; floors and rooms can override.

style.description: The project's default architectural style; floors and rooms can override.

registryVersions.description: Per-registry version the project was last saved against, keyed by registry name (for example "elementTypes" or "finishes"). Drives registry-aware migration. See the design specification, section 3.4.

PeriodId.description: References an entry in the PeriodRegistry. Validated at the registry boundary, not by this alias.

StyleTag.description: A style tag: a StyleRegistry id, optionally marked as the vernacular variant of an academic style. The `vernacular` modifier is meaningful only when the referenced Style entry declares `hasVernacularVariant`; it is ignored on entries that are themselves vernacular forms.

StyleId.description: References an entry in the StyleRegistry. Validated at the registry boundary, not by this alias.

SchemaVersion.description: Monotonically increasing project-schema version; drives the migration chain.

Extensions.description: Third-party extension data. Keys are reverse-DNS namespaces (for example "com.example.solar"); values are arbitrary JSON owned by that namespace. The CORE schema validates this as an open object so namespaced data never breaks CORE validation. See docs/specs/2026-06-10-vernacular-floor-plan-format.md, section 6.3.

elevation.description: Elevation of the finished floor surface, in millimeters.

defaultCeilingHeight.description: Default ceiling height for rooms on this floor, in millimeters.

periodOverride.description: Explicit period override; absent means inherit the project period.

styleOverride.description: Explicit style override; absent means inherit the project style.

Point.description: A point in floor-plan space, in millimeters. x increases rightward, y increases upward.

source.description: Discriminated reference to the underlay's source content (content-addressed, ADR-0007).

UnderlaySource.description: Reference + per-kind data for an underlay's source content (content-addressed, ADR-0007).

AssetReference.description: Content-addressed reference to an external asset. See ADR-0007.

offset.description: World position (millimeters) of the underlay's pixel origin (top-left).

millimetersPerPixel.description: World millimeters per source image pixel; the calibrated scale.

rotation.description: Rotation in radians about `offset`. Positive values rotate clockwise on screen, matching the image's downward-y pixel axes; 0 for an axis-aligned underlay.

position.description: Millimeters from the host wall start to the opening center, along the wall.

sillHeight.description: Height of the sill above the finished floor, in millimeters.

hinge.description: Which jamb anchors the leaf, as the host-wall end nearer it.

facing.description: Sign of the wall's left-hand normal the leaf opens toward.

WallEnd.description: One end of a wall: its start point or its end point.

start.description: First measured point, in world millimeters.

end.description: Second measured point, in world millimeters.

offset.description: Perpendicular offset of the dimension line from the measured segment, in millimeters. 0 places the dimension line on the segment.

position.description: Plan position of the stair origin, in world millimeters.

Stair.description: A stair joining two floors. Stairs live in a top-level, floor-spanning array rather than on a single floor because they connect two floors; see the design specification, sections 3.1 and 3.2.

StairRunType.description: How a stair run is shaped in plan; see the design specification, sections 3.1 and 3.2.

StairConnection.description: The pair of floors a stair joins. The run rises from `fromFloorId` to `toFloorId`; the type does not enforce any elevation ordering, so keeping the direction sensible is the caller's responsibility.

name.description: User-entered display name for the room; absent means geometry only (no name).

customPolygon.description: Replacement boundary for cases where wall topology cannot infer a room (porch, L-shaped sub-zone).

purpose.description: Primary room purpose, a RoomPurposeRegistry id. Absent means untagged.

subPurpose.description: Optional finer-grained free-text purpose label (for example "Silver Pantry"). Never required.

periodOverride.description: Explicit period override; absent means inherit the floor or project period.

styleOverride.description: Explicit style override; absent means inherit the floor or project style.

ceilingHeight.description: Explicit ceiling-height override in millimeters; absent means inherit the floor default.

RoomOverride.description:
User-supplied metadata for a derived room, stored separately from geometry. Held in `Project.roomOverrides`, keyed by `roomKey(room)` (the sorted bounding-wall-id string the room derivation encodes in `Room.id`). An absent map means no overrides.

The old-house architectural vocabulary fields (`purpose`, `subPurpose`, `periodOverride`, `styleOverride`) live here, each optional, alongside the room name and custom polygon.

RoomPurposeId.description: References an entry in the RoomPurposeRegistry. Validated at the registry boundary, not by this alias.

periods.description: Optional chronological-period tags, referencing the PeriodRegistry (ADR-0046).

ProjectPalette.description: A project-local, user-editable palette (design spec 3.1: palettes[] is project-local; the read-only bundled palettes live in the PaletteRegistry).

NamedColor.description: A color with a required accessible name (design spec 7.4: every chip carries its name). It lives here, beside Color, so both the palette registry and the project-local palette model import it from one home and the import direction stays core/registries and core/model both depending on core/color.

Color.description: A stored color in the three forms the design specification (section 7.4) requires: OKLab (canonical, used for all math), sRGB hex (display and serialization), and an optional originalSpec source identifier (for example a descriptive source color code the user records). The triple is always consistent because it is only built through the constructors below.

OkLab.description: A color in the OKLab perceptual space: L lightness (0..1), a and b chroma axes.

SurfaceTreatment.description: A surface treatment. Solid color is the only built variant; the discriminated `kind` is the extension seam for future `tiled-image` and `pattern` variants (ADR-0052).

northBearing.description: Angle from plan-up to true north, in radians (matching UnderlayPlacement.rotation).

LatLong.description: A geographic location in decimal degrees.

footprint.description: Footprint polygon in the plan frame, in world millimeters.

Obstruction.description: A top-down massing footprint of a nearby structure or tree, with a height. A non-rendering placeholder (design spec 3.1 and Phase 6); the Phase-8 solar lighting provider would later consume these for obstruction shadows.
